evals.prompt: I just cloned this Odoo repo, help me set up context
evals.expected_output: Skill walks the 9-step workflow; writes .odoo-ai/context.md with detected version + modules; updates .gitignore

evals.prompt: first time using Odoo for this project
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers; runs 9-step flow; produces context file

evals.prompt: I want to pin Odoo 17 and the viindoo_17 profile for this repo
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers; uses 17.0 + standard_viindoo_17 as defaults from prompt; confirms with user; writes context

evals.prompt: review this PR (working dir is a fresh Odoo repo with no .odoo-ai/)
evals.expected_output: Either: (a) onboard fires proactively due to missing context, OR (b) odoo-code-review fires first but immediately suggests running onboard. Phase A acceptance: skill description should make case (a) preferred but case (b) acceptable.

evals.prompt: Initialize Odoo context for a new project
review this code (context exists, last_updated 1 day ago)
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT fire - context is fresh; odoo-code-review should fire directly. Onboard Step 0 pre-flight catches this case.

evals.prompt: Write a computed field for sale.order
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT fire - specific intent matches odoo-coding. If context is missing, odoo-coding may suggest onboard but onboard itself does not auto-fire on this prompt.
Skill does NOT fire - non-Odoo intent.

notes: Full Mode 5 trigger optimization scheduled for Phase D AC-D6, combined with odoo-intake. Phase A acceptance verified via inline walkthrough (see main agent's A4 report). Test case #4 is the trickiest - it tests proactive onboard triggering when context is missing. Phase B will wire all skills to check for missing context.
